title: cerastium moesiacum subsp. glutinosum
intro: Cerastium moesiacum subsp. glutinosum, also known as the sticky mouse-ear chickweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating wounds, skin irritations, and infections. The health benefits of Cerastium moesiacum subsp. glutinosum include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies such as infusions, decoctions, and salves, which are used topically to treat various health issues.
